texts.full_text: imate = TRUE ), sliderInput(inputIterations, label = "Iterations:", value = 1, min = 1, max = 10, step = 1, animate = TRUE ), sliderInput(inputId = "sliders", label = "Sliders:", value = 1, min = 1, max = 30, step = 1, animate = TRUE ) ), mainPanel( tabsetPanel(type = "tabs", tabPanel("Simulation", plotOutput("plot", height = 800, width = 1000)), tabPanel("Plot", plotOutput("plot", height = 800, width = 1000)), tabPanel("Table", tableOutput("table")) ) ) ) ) server <- function(input, output) { output$plot <- renderPlot({ # Plotting function here }) output$table <- renderTable({ # Table generation function here }) } shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server) In this code, you can customize the values for the 'sliderInput' function by specifying the 'value' argument. For example, for the "temp" slider, you can set the initial value to 1, and for the "sliders" slider, you can set the initial value to 1 and the maximum value to 30.
